description: This is a bright-field transmission electron microscopy (TEM) image of Fe-20at.%Al alloy after 2-3% room temperature compressive deformation, with a scale bar of 0.2 μm. The observation is performed under a zone axis near [0 0 1], using imaging g vector 1 1 0 marked by the arrow. The microstructure shows straight screw dislocations, some of which are loosely paired, narrow edge dipole segments, and abundant small dipole loops. The tested Fe-20at.%Al alloy has a bcc structure with short-range order, and its deformation is dominated by glide of single dislocations with Burgers vector (1/2)<1 1 1>. The observed dipole loops and segments are confirmed to be generated by trailing from gliding screw dislocations. This figure supports the conclusion that single dislocation motion and dipole formation contribute to work hardening in low-Al content Fe-Al alloys.
